behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/LLM-DEP: failure is in real STT diarization quality, not local parsing. Candidate tests: low(Unit)=feed fixed transcript with labels (catches parser bugs, misses STT label loss); current(Agentic)=real multi-speaker audio to real STT, assert speaker-labeled transcript supports correct attribution (catches diarization/label-drop failures); high(Workflow)=full pipeline run (also catches downstream issues but is above minimum for this behavior). Rejection gate for Workflow: behavior text targets STT output preservation itself, not multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real meeting audio clip with known speakers and ground-truth utterance ownership; call production STT API; assert each sampled utterance retains a stable speaker label and labels are sufficient for downstream attendee attribution.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is parser logic over transcript text/schema mapping, no required external boundary. Candidate tests: low(Unit)=pure function with transcript fixtures (catches missed regex/grammar cases); current(Unit)=table-driven parse cases incl. edge punctuation and speaker prefixes; high(Integration)=wire parser into service layer (adds little for stated failure). Rejection gate for Integration+: behavior text is explicitly about extraction into input structure, with transcript already available.
behaviors.test_description: Run table-driven unit tests on the parsing function with realistic transcript snippets; assert exact assignee/task pairs and schema fields produced, including negative cases where no explicit assignment exists.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real model output coverage of assigned attendees. Candidate tests: low(Integration)=stub LLM returns perfect JSON (catches orchestration only, misses omission by real model); current(Agentic)=real LLM on transcript with known assignments, assert per-assignee action-item coverage; high(Workflow)=full upload->transcribe->summarize run (broader than needed). Rejection gate for Workflow: behavior is summary coverage quality, can be tested at summarization stage with transcript input.
behaviors.test_description: Provide transcript fixtures with explicit assignments to multiple attendees and invoke the real summarization model; assert output action_items includes at least one item per assigned attendee.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is deterministic routing logic after decisions are already identified. Candidate tests: low(Unit)=map identified decision objects to output schema (catches field-routing bugs); current(Unit)=unit tests for routing precedence/conflicts; high(Integration)=run through summarizer service (not required for this failure mode). Rejection gate for Agentic+: behavior text says 'identified decisions', disqualifying dependence on model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the decision-routing function with pre-identified decision inputs; assert they populate `decisions[]` and are absent from narrative-only fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: requires real model to correctly detect/promote decisions into the right section; stubbed outputs hide this. Candidate tests: low(Integration)=mock model returns decisions correctly (misses real extraction failures); current(Agentic)=real model on transcripts with known key decisions, assert presence in `decisions`; high(Workflow)=full pipeline E2E (broader than minimum). Rejection gate for Workflow: behavior can be isolated to transcript->summary decision placement quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcript samples with annotated key decisions; call the production summarizer model; assert each annotated key decision appears in `decisions` and not only in free-form narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP + LLM-DEP: multiple segment-level transcribe/summarize calls and merge step; failures emerge across sequential agentic handoffs (context loss, merge incoherence). Candidate tests: low(Integration)=stub segment processors and verify split/loop/merge wiring (misses real cross-segment coherence/model drift); current(Workflow)=real long audio through segmented pipeline, assert sequential processing and coherent merged output; high(System)=real infra only without model-quality checks misses coherence regressions. Rejection gate for lower levels: behavior explicitly requires 'single coherent summary' after sequential segmented processing.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60 minute meeting recording through production segmentation mode; verify segment count/order, successful per-segment model calls, and merged final summary preserves cross-segment continuity of action items and decisions.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA + MULTI-STEP + LLM-DEP: true E2E involves real upload path, STT, LLM summarization, and delivery contract across multiple stages. Candidate tests: low(Integration)=in-process fakes for STT/LLM (misses real API/runtime failures); current(Workflow)=real recording through full production pipeline with no human fixes (catches orchestration, external API, and model-stage failures); high(none)=current already maximal needed. Rejection gate for lower levels: behavior text explicitly requires full pipeline on a real meeting recording.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end test using a real meeting audio file in a production-like environment; assert successful completion of upload, transcription, summarization, and structured output delivery with required schema fields and no manual retries/interventions.
